bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Structural Bioinformatics & Protein Science

AI-curated selection: protein structure prediction, molecular dynamics, docking, and structure-based function annotation.

59 papers curated · High: 2 · Medium: 45 · Low: 12 · Updated: 2026-07-10

Synthetic and systems biotechnology 2027-Jan-01 Medium

Improved catalytic efficiency of P450 OleP for converting lithocholic acid into murideoxycholic and ursodeoxycholic acids through semi-rational and rational design.

Researchers engineered the P450 enzyme OleP to more efficiently convert lithocholic acid into the therapeutic bile acids murideoxycholic acid and ursodeoxycholic acid. They developed a high-throughput screening method and identified the S240A mutant, which significantly improved catalytic efficiency for potential large-scale production.

synthetic-biologydrug-discoverymethodology
ACS omega 2026-Jun-30 Low

3D-QSAR Modeling of the Anti-Inflammatory Activity of 28 Rare Biflavones.

This study investigates the anti-inflammatory potential of 28 rare biflavones with an underexplored C2'-C6″ linkage, using computational 3D-QSAR modeling. The findings identify key structural features that contribute to anti-inflammatory activity, offering a foundation for designing safer alternatives to current anti-inflammatory drugs.

drug-discoverymachine-learningmethodology
ACS omega 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Design, Synthesis and Biological Effects Studies of Novel EGFR Inhibitors Targeting Wild-Type and Mutant EGFR (EGFR-L858R and EGFR-L858R/T790M).

Researchers designed and synthesized new EGFR inhibitors that target both normal and mutant forms of the EGFR protein, which is important in lung cancer. The most promising compounds, particularly those with 3,4-dichloro and 2,4-dichloro substitutions, showed strong anti-cancer activity against lung cancer cells while being less toxic to healthy cells. This work provides potential new leads for treating lung cancers that have developed resistance to current EGFR-targeted therapies.

drug-discoverycancermethodology
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-24 Medium

Surface-specific film assembly of a Vibrio cholerae adhesin-derived peptide modulated by environmental salts.

This study investigates how different environmental salts affect the surface-specific film assembly of a short peptide derived from the Vibrio cholerae biofilm adhesin Bap1. The peptide, SYWFFGWHTK, shows strong underwater adhesion that can be modulated by the ionic environment, providing insights into bioinspired adhesive design.

microbiologystructural-biologybiomaterials
Chemical science 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Ultrafast excited-state proton transfer dynamics using linearized pair-density functional theory.

This paper presents linearized pair-density functional theory (L-PDFT) as an efficient method for simulating excited-state bond-rearrangement dynamics, such as ultrafast proton transfer. The approach balances accuracy and computational feasibility, enabling molecular dynamics simulations of photoinduced reactions that involve significant electronic structure changes.

methodologystructural-biology
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Simulation of cell-size systems at long timescales with flexible protein structures.

This paper presents a new docking-based simulation method that uses Markov Chain Monte Carlo sampling to model protein behavior in crowded cellular environments, achieving speeds several orders of magnitude faster than traditional molecular dynamics. The approach leverages recent AI-driven advances in protein structure prediction to enable structure-based modeling of cellular phenomena at long timescales.

structural-biologymethodologymachine-learning
Chemical science 2026-Jun-18 Medium

HCGT-PL: a heterogeneous contrastive graph transformer unifying protein-ligand affinity prediction and structure-based virtual screening.

This paper introduces HCGT-PL, a new deep learning framework that represents protein-ligand complexes as directed heterogeneous graphs to improve both binding affinity prediction and virtual screening. The model uses relation-specific attention and unsupervised contrastive learning to capture complex molecular interactions, outperforming existing methods on diverse benchmarks.

machine-learningdeep-learningdrug-discovery
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Integrative immunoinformatics and structural modeling for the rational design of a multi-epitope vaccine candidate against human cytomegalovirus.

This study used computational methods to design a multi-epitope vaccine candidate against human cytomegalovirus (CMV), for which no licensed vaccine exists. By screening viral proteins for epitopes that stimulate B cells and T cells, the researchers constructed a vaccine construct and validated its stability and immunogenicity through structural modeling and molecular dynamics simulations.

immunologystructural-biologydrug-discovery
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Decoding cytokine interactions for psoriasis therapy through computational repurposing of antihypertensive drugs.

This study computationally repurposes FDA-approved antihypertensive drugs for psoriasis therapy by analyzing their interactions with key inflammatory cytokines. The findings suggest that certain antihypertensives may inhibit IL-6, IL-1β, IL-17A, and IFN-γ, offering a cost-effective alternative to current treatments with potentially fewer side effects.

drug-discoveryimmunologymachine-learning
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Discovery of indolylchalcone benzenesulfonamides as selective inhibitors of tumor-associated carbonic anhydrase IX and XII.

This study reports the design and synthesis of novel indolylchalcone benzenesulfonamide compounds that selectively inhibit tumor-associated carbonic anhydrase IX and XII, which are potential targets for cancer therapy. The compounds show promise as selective anticancer agents by targeting these specific enzymes.

drug-discoverycancer
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Low

Molecular dynamics investigation of interfacial energy and mechanical behavior in braid-reinforced hollow fiber membranes.

This study uses molecular dynamics simulations to analyze the interfacial energy and mechanical behavior of braid-reinforced hollow fiber membranes made from cellulose acetate and polyacrylonitrile. The researchers found that homogeneous configurations have the highest interfacial energy, while heterogeneous hybrids show lower adhesion, providing insights for designing stronger composite membranes.

structural-biologymaterials-science
eLife 2026-Jul-08 High

Lenacapavir-induced lattice hyperstabilization is central to HIV-1 capsid failure at the nuclear pore complex and in the cytoplasm.

This study reveals that the HIV-1 drug lenacapavir works by hyperstabilizing the viral capsid lattice, which prevents the capsid from properly interacting with nuclear pore complexes and blocks nuclear entry. Using simulations and live-cell imaging, the authors show that while drug-damaged capsids can still dock at the nuclear pore, only intact capsids can enter the nucleus, explaining lenacapavir's mechanism of action.

structural-biologydrug-discoveryvirology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Theoretical study on N(sp2)+-C(sp2)-H···O hydrogen bonds in the context of drug discovery.

This study uses quantum-chemical calculations to show that N(sp2)⁺-C(sp2)-H···O hydrogen bonds involving pyridinium ions are strong and directional, contrary to the common view that C-H···O bonds are weak. The findings suggest these interactions could be leveraged in structure-based drug design to improve binding affinity.

drug-discoverystructural-biologymethodology
PLoS pathogens 2026-Jul-08 Medium

A structure-based epitope tagging approach identifies vulnerable sites on the malarial P36-P52 protein complex for antibody-mediated neutralization of Plasmodium sporozoites.

This study uses a structure-based epitope tagging approach to identify vulnerable sites on the P36-P52 protein complex of malaria sporozoites, which can be targeted by antibodies to neutralize the parasite before it infects the liver. The findings highlight new potential targets for malaria vaccine development beyond the currently targeted circumsporozoite protein.

structural-biologyimmunologydrug-discovery
Cell reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Structure of the Beat-Side complex reveals their interaction is required for neuromuscular system wiring.

This study reports the first structure of a Beat-Side protein complex (Beat-Vc bound to Side-VI), revealing a binding topology similar to PD-1-PD-L1 and other immunoglobulin superfamily receptors. The findings demonstrate that Beat-Side interactions are essential for proper neuromuscular system wiring in Drosophila, providing molecular insight into axon guidance and synaptic connectivity.

structural-biologyneuroscienceevolution
RSC advances 2026-Jul-07 Low

Design and computational evaluation of E-stilbene-bearing 1,3,4-oxadiazole derivatives as potential tyrosinase inhibitors using DFT, molecular docking and ADMET studies.

This study designed and tested new chemical compounds that inhibit tyrosinase, an enzyme involved in melanin production, with some showing strong activity at low concentrations. The most promising compounds were identified through computational modeling and lab experiments, suggesting potential applications in skin-lightening or anti-browning products.

drug-discoverymethodology
Cancer cell international 2026-Jul-07 Medium

ANGPTL4 contributes to osimertinib resistance through NDRG1-associated extracellular matrix remodeling in EGFR-mutant NSCLC organoid models.

This study uses 3D organoid models of EGFR-mutant non-small cell lung cancer to show that the secreted protein ANGPTL4 drives resistance to the drug osimertinib by remodeling the extracellular matrix via NDRG1. The findings highlight a new resistance mechanism and demonstrate the value of organoid models for studying tumor microenvironment interactions.

cancerdrug-discoverytranscriptomics
Communications biology 2026-Jul-07 Medium

The influence of ligands on AlphaFold3 prediction of cryptic pockets.

This study evaluates AlphaFold3's ability to predict cryptic pockets, which are hidden binding sites that become accessible only after a protein changes shape. The researchers found that AlphaFold3 can generally reproduce the large-scale conformational changes needed for these pockets to form, and when provided with a ligand that binds to a cryptic site, it predominantly predicts the open, ligand-bound conformation. This suggests AlphaFold3 could be a useful tool for identifying druggable cryptic pockets, though the work is an incremental validation of an existing method.

structural-biologydrug-discoverymachine-learning
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-07 Low

Ketodarolutamide may interact with the receptor-binding domain of the SARS-CoV-2 spike glycoprotein: the importance of halogenated benzonitrile.

This study used computational docking to screen FDA-approved drugs for binding to the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein's receptor-binding domain (RBD), identifying ketodarolutamide as a promising candidate. The compound's halogenated benzonitrile group appears important for binding, suggesting a potential repurposing opportunity for COVID-19 treatment.

drug-discoverystructural-biologymachine-learning
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Therapeutic potential of Citrus medica L. (cv. 'Liscia' and cv. 'Rugosa') phytocompounds targeting biofilm formation, quorum sensing, and antioxidant defense mechanisms.

This study uses network pharmacology and computational modeling to investigate how bioactive compounds from two varieties of Citrus medica (citron) might simultaneously target biofilm formation, quorum sensing, and antioxidant defense mechanisms. The findings suggest that these phytocompounds could serve as multi-target therapeutic agents for persistent infections and chronic inflammatory diseases.

drug-discoverysystems-biologymicrobiology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Pangenome-guided immunoinformatics design and in silico characterization of a multi-epitope vaccine candidate against Acinetobacter baumannii with nanoparticle assembly potential.

Researchers used pangenome analysis of over 700 genomes to identify conserved surface antigens in the drug-resistant pathogen Acinetobacter baumannii, then designed a multi-epitope vaccine candidate using machine learning and structural vaccinology. The vaccine incorporates self-assembling nanoparticle technology and dual adjuvants, showing promising immunogenic potential in computational simulations. This study provides a computational blueprint for developing a vaccine against a critical pathogen where none currently exists.

machine-learningimmunologyvaccine
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-07 Low

Network pharmacology and in silico analysis of Azadirachta indica phytoconstituents reveal potential hepatoprotective targets and mechanisms.

This study uses network pharmacology and bioinformatics to identify how compounds from neem leaf extract may protect the liver from injury. By analyzing chemical profiles and simulating interactions with biological networks, the authors pinpoint potential molecular targets and pathways involved in hepatoprotection.

network-analysissystems-biologydrug-discovery
Scientific data 2026-Jul-07 High

A comprehensive dataset of 32 million pentapeptide structures for high-throughput virtual screening.

This paper presents an open dataset of 32 million 3D structures covering all possible pentapeptide sequences, generated using an automated computational workflow. This resource enables high-throughput virtual screening and structure-based analysis of small peptides without requiring experimental structure determination.

databasestructural-biologydrug-discovery
Chinese medicine 2026-Jul-06 Medium

A systems herbal-to-molecule and transcriptomic strategy identifies emodin as an ESR1-targeting phytochemical driving thrombopoiesis.

This study uses an integrated herbal-to-molecule and transcriptomic approach to identify emodin, a compound from the herb Rhei Radix et Rhizoma, as a promoter of platelet production by targeting the ESR1 receptor. The findings suggest a potential new treatment for radiation-induced thrombocytopenia, a common side effect of radiotherapy.

transcriptomicsdrug-discoverysystems-biology
BMC microbiology 2026-Jul-06 Medium

A population-based retrospective machine learning study of COVID-19 severity using integrated clinical and viral genomic data in Jiangsu Province, China.

This study used machine learning to analyze clinical and viral genomic data from COVID-19 patients in Jiangsu Province, China, identifying key factors associated with severe disease. The findings highlight region-specific risk determinants that can aid in real-world patient stratification and public health planning during the endemic phase.

machine-learningclinicalgenomics
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Molecular dynamics simulation of the atropine drug encapsulation inside protein origami icosahedral nanocages in physiological environment.

This study uses molecular dynamics simulations to investigate how the drug atropine can be encapsulated inside protein origami icosahedral nanocages for potential drug delivery applications. The simulations model the behavior of these nanocages in a physiological environment, providing insights into their stability and drug-loading capacity.

drug-discoverystructural-biologymethodology
NPJ systems biology and applications 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Identification of phillyrin as a PPAR-γ ligand through network pharmacology and molecular modeling.

This study identifies phillyrin, a compound from Forsythia suspensa, as a direct ligand of PPAR-γ, a key target for improving insulin sensitivity. Using network pharmacology and molecular modeling, the authors demonstrate that phillyrin binds to PPAR-γ, potentially explaining its ability to improve insulin resistance in obesity. The findings suggest phillyrin could be a safer alternative to synthetic PPAR-γ agonists for treating type 2 diabetes.

drug-discoverysystems-biologymethodology
Nature communications 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Time-resolved Coulomb explosion imaging of a photochemical ring opening reaction.

This study demonstrates that time-resolved Coulomb explosion imaging combined with molecular dynamics simulations can directly visualize the ultrafast nuclear motion during the photoinduced ring-opening of furan, a prototypical cyclic organic molecule. This approach resolves long-standing controversies about reaction mechanisms in photochemical ring-opening processes.

methodologystructural-biology
eLife 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Lipid packing contributes to the confinement of caveolae to the plasma membrane.

This study reveals that lipid packing, a fundamental property of cell membranes, plays a critical role in confining caveolae (small membrane invaginations) to the plasma membrane. The authors show that the compound Dyngo-4a inserts into the membrane and disrupts this confinement by altering lipid packing, providing mechanistic insight into how membrane lipid organization directly influences caveolae function.

structural-biologycell-biology
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jul-06 Medium

ScrambleBench: a workflow for comparative assessment of structure-based de novo generative models.

This paper introduces ScrambleBench, a standardized workflow for evaluating structure-based de novo generative models in drug discovery. The tool addresses the need for consistent and comparable assessment of generative AI models, which currently lack uniform evaluation criteria, to determine their robustness for medicinal chemistry integration.

drug-discoverymachine-learningtool
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Metastable multimeric G-quadruplex 2'FY-RNA aptamers that selectively bind pyoverdines.

This study reports the selection of two 2'FY-RNA aptamers that specifically bind to the bacterial siderophore pyoverdine-Pf5, increasing its fluorescence. The aptamers form metastable G-quadruplex structures that are stabilized by target binding, and they recognize both the chromophore and peptide portions of pyoverdines. This work provides a new class of RNA-based sensors for bacterial detection and siderophore research.

structural-biologytoolmicrobiology
RSC advances 2026-Jul-03 Low

A molecular dynamics study on anticorrosion properties of graphene/epoxy nanocomposites in NaCl medium.

This study uses molecular dynamics and density functional theory to model how different graphene derivatives (pristine graphene, graphene oxide, reduced graphene oxide, and functionalized graphene oxide) in epoxy coatings protect iron from corrosion in a sodium chloride environment. The findings provide insights into the anticorrosion mechanisms of graphene/epoxy nanocomposites, which could inform the design of better protective coatings.

materials-sciencemethodology
RSC advances 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Computationally optimized molecularly imprinted electrochemical sensor based on biomass-derived biochar for paclobutrazol analysis in Radix Angelicae Sinensis.

This study developed a novel electrochemical sensor for detecting the pesticide paclobutrazol in a medicinal herb, using a sustainable biochar derived from the herb's own stalks and a computationally designed molecular imprinting layer. The sensor achieved high sensitivity and selectivity, offering a promising tool for ensuring the safety and quality of herbal products.

toolmethodology
RSC advances 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Structural and biochemical insights into the antibiofilm activity of cationic lipoamino acid ester against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus.

This study reports the first single-crystal X-ray structure of a cationic lipoamino acid ester (QPyN14Ge) with antibiofilm activity against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. The structure reveals a highly ordered packing arrangement with tail-to-tail interdigitation of hydrophobic layers, providing insights into its solid-state properties and potential for structure-based drug design against biofilm-associated infections.

structural-biologydrug-discoverymicrobiology
RSC advances 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Engineering intrinsically radiolabeled nanoparticles for imaging and therapy: a decade of design strategies and insights.

This review summarizes a decade of design strategies for intrinsically radiolabeled nanoparticles, which incorporate radionuclides directly into the nanoparticle matrix to improve stability and preserve nanomaterial properties for cancer imaging and therapy. The authors discuss mechanisms like lattice doping and isotopic substitution, highlighting how this approach overcomes limitations of conventional chelator-based methods.

nanotechnologydrug-discoveryreview
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-03 Medium

N-MIX: an in silico framework for predicting ADAM10-mediated substrate cleavage sites through structural and spatial analysis of protease-substrate interactions.

This study presents N-MIX, a computational framework that uses AlphaFold2-predicted 3D structures of ADAM10-substrate complexes to identify key structural determinants of substrate cleavage, overcoming the limitations of traditional sequence-based motif analysis. By analyzing hydrogen bond interactions and spatial features, the framework accurately predicts ADAM10-mediated cleavage sites, offering a new approach for studying protease specificity in cancer, inflammation, and neurodegeneration.

structural-biologymachine-learningtool
Journal of structural biology 2026-Jul-03 Medium

MLAC: MicroED-assisted ligand structure analysis in complexes and its application to hERG-ligand complexes.

MLAC is a new integrative framework that combines microcrystal electron diffraction (MicroED) with cryo-EM to accurately determine small-molecule ligand structures in protein-ligand complexes, addressing a key challenge in structure-based drug discovery. The method was successfully applied to hERG-ligand complexes, demonstrating its utility for reliable ligand identification when initial models are lacking.

structural-biologydrug-discoverymethodology
Journal of dairy science 2026-Jul-03 Low

Invited review: Manufacturing Whey Protein Colloidal Particles via Liquid Antisolvent Precipitation Method: Particle Formation Mechanism and Ingredient Functionality Aspects.

This review examines the liquid antisolvent (LAS) precipitation method for manufacturing whey protein particles, which offers tunable structures and functionalities compared to conventional heat-induced aggregation. The method works by rapidly reducing solvent quality to create protein supersaturation and aggregation under ambient conditions.

methodologyreview
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-03 Medium

TEDLH: Domain HMMs for sensitive detection of remote homologues.

The authors created a library of profile Hidden Markov models (TEDLH) based on domain annotations from the Encyclopedia of Domains (TED), which uses consensus structural methods on AlphaFold protein structures. TEDLH enables sensitive detection of remote protein homologues through sequence and profile searches, aiding the study of domain families and evolution.

protein-structuretoolmethodology
RSC advances 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Molecular modeling of 7-propanamide benzoxaboroles as CPSF3 inhibitors through docking-based 3D-QSAR and molecular dynamics simulations.

This study uses computational methods (docking-based 3D-QSAR and molecular dynamics simulations) to explore how 7-propanamide benzoxaboroles inhibit the mRNA processing enzyme CPSF3, a potential anti-cancer target. The work provides structural insights into the binding of these compounds, which could aid in designing more potent CPSF3 inhibitors.

drug-discoverystructural-biologymachine-learning
Biophysical journal 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Structure-based simulations of the full Flock House virus capsid reveal pathways and energetics of an infection-critical peptide externalization event.

This study uses a coarse-grained molecular dynamics model to simulate the entire Flock House virus capsid, revealing how a critical internal peptide becomes exposed during infection. The simulations show that the peptide externalization is a rare, cooperative event driven by specific structural rearrangements across the capsid. This work provides a mechanistic understanding of a key step in viral infection that is difficult to observe experimentally.

structural-biologymethodologymachine-learning
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Integrating machine learning and structure-based simulations to prioritise Z4P as a mutation-resilient IRE1α inhibitor for breast cancer.

This study used machine learning and molecular simulations to identify Z4P as a potential IRE1α inhibitor for breast cancer that may be resilient to drug resistance mutations. The approach combined computational methods to prioritize compounds that are both potent and non-toxic.

machine-learningdrug-discoverycancer
Veterinary medicine and science 2026-Jul-01 Low

Computational and Experimental Development of a Novel Multi-Epitope Vaccine Candidate Against Bovine Leukaemia Virus.

This study uses computational methods to design a multi-epitope vaccine against Bovine Leukaemia Virus (BLV), an economically important cattle disease with no licensed vaccine. The authors identified immunogenic epitopes from BLV proteins and constructed a vaccine candidate predicted to be stable and immunogenic, though experimental validation is still needed.

immunologydrug-discoveryveterinary
PNAS nexus 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Investigation and computational prediction of gating pore currents in NaV1.2 mutations across clinical phenotypes.

This study investigates how specific mutations in the voltage sensor domain of the NaV1.2 ion channel cause gating pore currents, which are linked to various clinical conditions. Using computational predictions and experimental validation, the authors show that mutations at positions R220 and R223 disrupt normal channel gating, leading to pathological leak currents. The findings provide a mechanistic understanding of how these mutations contribute to different disease severities.

structural-biologymachine-learningclinical
Food science & nutrition 2026-Jul-01 Low

Studying and Simulating the Impact of Plasma Activated Water (PAW) on Salmonella in Almond Products: A Molecular Dynamics Investigation.

This study investigates the use of plasma-activated water (PAW) to eliminate Salmonella contamination on almonds, finding that specific operational parameters like water flow rate and gas mixture can effectively reduce the pathogen. The research combines experimental methods with molecular dynamics simulations to understand the underlying mechanisms. This work offers a potential non-thermal decontamination approach for food safety applications.

microbiology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

OrgNet+: towards robust protein stability prediction with convolutional neural networks.

This study introduces OrgNet+, a 3D convolutional neural network that improves protein stability prediction by accounting for conformational flexibility, addressing a key limitation of existing structure-based methods that rely on single static structures. The model demonstrates more robust and stable predictions across different protein conformations, advancing the reliability of computational tools for protein engineering.

machine-learningprotein-structuretool
CNS neuroscience & therapeutics 2026-Jul-01 Low

Potential Mechanisms of Trimetazidine and Coenzyme Q10 Against Antipsychotic-Induced Myocarditis: A Network Pharmacology, Molecular Docking, and Molecular Dynamics Simulation Study.

This study uses computational methods to explore how trimetazidine and coenzyme Q10 might protect the heart from damage caused by antipsychotic drugs. By analyzing networks of interacting proteins and simulating how these molecules bind to targets, the authors identify potential mechanisms for their cardioprotective effects. The findings suggest these compounds could be developed as adjunctive therapies to prevent a rare but serious side effect of antipsychotic treatment.

network-analysisdrug-discoverymethodology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

seq2ribo: structure-aware integration of machine learning and simulation to predict ribosome location profiles from RNA sequences.

seq2ribo is a new computational method that combines machine learning and simulation to predict ribosome location profiles directly from RNA sequences, without needing experimental data like Ribo-seq. This enables its use in de novo sequence design, such as for mRNA vaccines, overcoming limitations of existing methods that require full genomic context. The approach improves upon simulation-only models by incorporating structure-aware features beyond just codon elongation times.

machine-learningmethodologysynthetic-biology
Journal of biological engineering 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Structure-function relationship in FGF signaling: rational design of highly stable FGF2 variants and their effect on cell growth, metabolism, and survival.

This study investigates how structural modifications can enhance the thermal stability of FGF2, a key growth factor used in stem cell and regeneration research. The authors designed stable FGF2 variants and tested their effects on cell growth, metabolism, and survival, aiming to overcome the protein's rapid degradation at body temperature. This work could improve the practical utility of FGF2 in experimental and therapeutic settings.

structural-biologyprotein-structuretool
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

KASSPer: Kinase Active Site Structure Prediction using Protein and Ligand Language Models and Its Application to Virtual Screening.

KASSPer is a new computational method that uses protein and ligand language models to predict kinase active site structures, enabling more accurate virtual screening by accounting for the multiple conformations kinases can adopt. This approach overcomes the limitations of traditional rigid-receptor virtual screening without the high computational cost of ensemble methods.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytool
Chemical biology & drug design 2026-Jul-01 Medium

In Silico Drug Repositioning Identifies SYK Kinase Inhibitors as Potential Neuroprotective Agents for Ataxia-Telangiectasia.

This study uses computational drug repositioning to identify FDA-approved SYK kinase inhibitors as potential treatments for Ataxia-Telangiectasia, a rare neurodegenerative disease. The authors developed an in silico pipeline combining deep learning-based molecular docking to screen for compounds that could block the aberrant SYK signaling linked to neuroinflammation in this disorder.

drug-discoverymachine-learningneuroscience
Antibody therapeutics 2026-Jul-01 Low

Preventing physical and chemical degradation of the LABL-Fc-MOGR5, a bifunctional peptide inhibitor, with formulation development approaches.

This study identifies and addresses the physical instability of a bifunctional peptide inhibitor for multiple sclerosis, showing that formulation approaches can prevent its rapid precipitation. The work provides a path forward for developing this therapeutic candidate.

drug-discovery
Frontiers in pharmacology 2026--01 Low

Uncovering antioxidant and anti-breast cancer potential metabolite from streptomyces strain sp. VITAMB using integrated experimental and computational approaches.

This study identifies a yellow pigment-producing Streptomyces strain from paddy soil with antioxidant and anti-breast cancer activity, using both lab experiments and computational modeling. The findings suggest this bacterial metabolite could be a promising candidate for developing natural antioxidant and anticancer therapies.

microbiologydrug-discoverycancer
Computational and structural biotechnology journal 2026--01 Medium

Experimental and Mechanistic Validation of PARP1pred for Identifying Potent Leads.

This study validates the PARP1pred machine learning model for identifying new PARP1 inhibitors, which are important for treating certain cancers. The model's predictions were confirmed through biochemical and cellular experiments, showing it can find effective drug leads in new chemical space.

machine-learningdrug-discoverycancer
Frontiers in immunology 2026--01 Medium

Identification and experimental validation of CD74, PGLYRP1, and TXN as potential biomarkers in rheumatoid arthritis: an integrative bulk and ScRNA-seq study.

This study identified CD74, PGLYRP1, and TXN as potential biomarkers for rheumatoid arthritis by integrating bulk and single-cell RNA sequencing data, and experimentally validated their expression. These biomarkers may improve diagnostic specificity and sensitivity for RA, and the findings suggest they play roles in immune cell infiltration and inflammatory pathways.

transcriptomicssingle-cellimmunology
Frontiers in nutrition 2026--01 Medium

Integrated multi-omics analysis reveals distinct microbiota-metabolite signatures and a novel HCN2-2-hydroxybutyric acid interaction in inflammatory bowel disease.

This study profiles gut microbiota and short-chain fatty acids in ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease, identifying distinct metabolite signatures for each subtype. It also uncovers a novel interaction between the ion channel HCN2 and 2-hydroxybutyric acid, suggesting a potential molecular mechanism linking microbial metabolites to host targets in inflammatory bowel disease.

microbiomemetabolomicsimmunology
International journal of nanomedicine 2026--01 Medium

Liposomal Nanoparticulate Drug Delivery Systems: Strategies to Destabilize Biological Membranes at the Target Tissue.

This review discusses liposomal nanoparticulate drug delivery systems (LNDDSs), which are clinically used nanomedicines for oncology and infectious diseases, and are now expanding to deliver nucleic acid therapeutics like RNA vaccines. It focuses on strategies to destabilize biological membranes at target tissues to improve selective delivery of therapeutic agents.

drug-discoveryreviewclinical
Drug design, development and therapy 2026--01 Medium

Design, Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of New Oxazolidinone Antimicrobial Agents with Low Monoamine Oxidase A Inhibition.

Researchers designed new oxazolidinone antibiotics that retain antibacterial activity against Gram-positive bacteria and Mycobacterium tuberculosis while reducing the dangerous side effect of monoamine oxidase A inhibition seen with the existing drug linezolid. This work aims to improve the safety profile of this important antibiotic class.

drug-discoverymicrobiology
Frontiers in chemistry 2026--01 Medium

Photo-responsive nanozymes: from photocatalytic mechanisms to precision therapy.

This review discusses how combining photocatalytic processes with enzyme-mimetic catalysis can create 'photo-responsive nanozymes' that overcome the limited efficiency and selectivity of conventional nanozymes. These hybrid materials enable spatiotemporally controlled catalytic activity for precision biomedical applications. The paper systematically outlines design strategies and core mechanisms involving photogenerated charge carriers.

drug-discoveryreview

AI-curated by bioHot. Summaries and significance ratings are AI-generated; verify before citing. · bioHot Home
